summary: Backend regression suite for the deployment-readiness perf refactor (N+1 -> batched _hydrate_posts, and projection fixes on /api/likes + /api/users/{uid}/friends). 12/12 pytest cases pass. PostOut contract is unchanged; nice toggle, comment add, profile posts, likes, friends and the mobile multipart 'hi' regression all return correct hydrated payloads.
critical_code_review_comments: _hydrate_posts (posts_router.py:45-138) is clean and correctly batches 5 collection round-trips (users, post_likes count agg, viewer reactions find, post_comments count agg) regardless of N. Single-post wrapper _hydrate_post still routes through the same code path so behavior is consistent for create_post and top-niced.

_attach_recent_comments still issues an UNPROJECTED users.find on author_uids (posts_router.py:235) — consider adding {'uid':1,'name':1,'profilePictureUrl':1,'_id':0} for consistency with the projection added elsewhere; minor perf nit, not a blocker.
social_router.py:354 my_likes_cursor projection (to_uid only, _id excluded) is correct; verified via /api/inbox path indirectly via /api/likes test.

social_router.py:169 matches.find projection ({user_a, user_b, friended_by, _id:0}) verified; /api/friends and /api/users/{uid}/friends return 200 with []. Empty case is the realistic one for a fresh test user; populated friend list flow wasn't exercised end-to-end here but the projection itself is correct.
test_credentials: Firebase signup via Identity Toolkit REST API with REACT_APP_FIREBASE_API_KEY from /app/frontend/.env. Test users prefixed TEST_i19_* and cleaned up via accounts:delete in fixture teardown.
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates 2 ephemeral Firebase users (user + viewer) and ~5 posts under them; teardown deletes the Firebase identities (Mongo user docs persist but are inert and prefixed TEST_).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test pattern: use Identity Toolkit signUp REST endpoint with REACT_APP_FIREBASE_API_KEY to mint a real Firebase ID token, then POST /api/auth/sync to upsert the user record. Backend is in Firebase mode (FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON=/app/backend/firebase-admin.json), so a properly signed ID token is required. See /app/backend/tests/test_iteration19.py for the _signup / _sync / _h helpers.
rca of the issue: N/A — pure perf refactor verification. No bugs found. The single initial test failure was a wrong test assertion (expected author.name to be populated by /auth/sync payload, but the endpoint sources name from token claims only) — assertion corrected; behavior under test is intact.
